name: Sant Miquel del Fai Valley Loop from Sant Feliu de Codines
name_original: Valle de Sant Miguel de Fall – circular desde Sant Feliu de Codines
description: Cycle a moderate 13.9-mile (22.4 km) loop through the scenic Vallès Oriental region, featuring the dramatic Cingles de Bertí cliffs.
region_intro: Road cycling around Sant Feliu De Codines features a varied landscape characterized by valleys, forested areas, and ascents towards elevated points like Coll de la Pullosa. The terrain includes winding roads through natural parks and past significant geological features. This area offers a mix of challenging climbs and more undulating routes suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Sant Feliu De Codines The most popular road cycling route is Sant Miquel del Fai Valley Loop from Sant Feliu de Codines, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic Sant Miquel del Fai Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Sant Miquel del Fai Road loop from Bigues i Riells del Fai, a difficult 55.5 miles (89.4 km) path. This route includes a significant climb to Coll de la Pullosa, offering extensive views. Local road cyclists also love the Secrets Café Gourmet loop from Santa Eulàlia de Ronçana, a 23.5 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Feliu De Codines is defined by valleys, forested sections, and mountain passes, with options for both beginner road cycling routes and advanced road bike climbs near Sant Feliu De Codines. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Sant Feliu De Codines are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Feliu De Codines's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Feliu De Codines?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the Sant Feliu De Codines area. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from moderate loops through valleys to challenging climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sant Feliu De Codines?
A: Yes, Sant Feliu De Codines offers options for beginner road cyclists. There are 5 routes classified as easy, providing a gentler introduction to the region's varied landscape, which includes undulating roads and forested sections.

Q: Where can I find challenging road bike climbs near Sant Feliu De Codines?
A: For advanced road cyclists seeking climbs, the region features ascents towards elevated points like Coll de la Pullosa. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7792529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Sant Miquel del Fai Road loop from Bigues i Riells del Fai&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a significant climb and offers extensive views.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7792529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Sant Miquel del Fai Road loop from Bigues i Riells del Fai&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 55.5 miles (89.4 km), providing a substantial challenge and extended exploration of the region.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect on road bike routes around Sant Feliu De Codines?
A: The road cycling routes often pass through areas with significant geological features and offer views of valleys and forested landscapes. Routes that ascend to higher points, such as the climb to Coll de la Pullosa, provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding terrain.

Q: Are there circular road bike routes available?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Sant Feliu De Codines are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995569210/sant-miquel-del-fai-valley-loop-from-sant-feliu-de-codines" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Valley Loop from Sant Feliu de Codines&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the scenic valley in a circular fashion.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Sant Feliu De Codines?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. Road cyclists often praise the varied landscape, which includes a mix of challenging climbs and undulating routes, and the opportunity to explore natural parks and winding roads.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see near the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore historical sites like castles. Notable nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentmenat Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanta Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1392292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Clascar Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features mountain peaks with highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2076587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puiggraciós Optical Telegraph Tower&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1439769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Creu Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Yes, some routes are known to pass through villages or areas with amenities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34631050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Secrets Café Gourmet loop from Santa Eulàlia de Ronçana&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named, suggesting it includes stops or passes by places for refreshments, leading through varied rural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Sant Feliu De Codines?
A: The region's climate generally allows for road cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, while summer can be warm, and winter offers opportunities for those seeking cooler conditions, especially on routes with less elevation.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes that offer a good balance of challenge and scenery?
A: Absolutely. The majority of routes in Sant Feliu De Codines are classified as moderate (24 out of 35). A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34631184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Road – Centelles–Sant Feliu Road loop from Sant Feliu de Codines&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a balanced ride with varied terrain and scenic views without being overly strenuous.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Valle de Sant Miguel de Fall cycling route?
faqs.answer: The circular route begins and ends in Sant Feliu de Codines. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires good fitness due to its 460 meters of elevation gain over 22.4 kilometers. While primarily a road cycling route, some segments may be unpaved and challenging for road bikes, potentially requiring lower tire pressure. It is not ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows roads through the Vallès Oriental region. While mostly paved, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and in 'bad conditions' for short distances. The area features diverse terrain including hills, pine and holm oak forests, and open green spaces.

faqs.question: What are the scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience fantastic views throughout the journey, especially as you approach the Sant Miquel del Fai area, known for its dramatic Cingles de Bertí cliffs. Notable points include the Winding Road Between Can Sants and Les Ferreries and the Sant Miquel del Fai Road. The destination, Sant Miquel del Fai, offers spectacular vistas of the Catalan mountain range, a monastery, and impressive waterfalls.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Vallès Oriental region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. To enjoy the natural landscapes and avoid extreme heat, spring and autumn are often ideal. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route that may include some unpaved sections. While there are no specific prohibitions against dogs on public roads, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a racebike route of this nature due to safety and comfort concerns for both the cyclist and the animal.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route or near the starting point?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sant Feliu de Codines, your starting point, offers opportunities to stop for coffee or a meal before or after your ride. The Fonda Safaja is a highlight along the route that serves as a restaurant.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for cycling in the Cingles de Bertí area?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on the public roads and trails that make up this route within the Cingles de Bertí area. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with official trails such as Els Tres Monts, Ronda Codinenca, Sender dels Miradors, and parts of the GR 5 and GR-177.

faqs.question: What makes the Sant Miquel del Fai area unique?
faqs.answer: Sant Miquel del Fai is a significant cultural and natural landmark. It's known for its unique landscape, characterized by the dramatic Cingles de Bertí cliffs, often described as a 'smaller version of the Great Canyon'. The area features a monastery and impressive waterfalls, perched atop a mountain, offering a spectacular vista.

faqs.question: What should I know about the road conditions on this route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, some segments may be unpaved and in 'bad conditions' for short distances. Cyclists on road bikes might consider lowering tire pressure for these sections to improve comfort and grip. Always be prepared for varied surfaces.
